currentArticle.title: Earth
currentArticle.subtitle: From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
currentArticle.summary: Earth is the third planet from the Sun and the only astronomical object known to harbor life. This is the most substantial of the Earth's layers. About 29.2% of Earth's surface is land consisting of continents and islands.

sections.heading: Formation and evolution
sections.content: The oldest material found in the Solar System is dated to 4.5682 billion years ago. By 4.54 billion years ago, the primordial Earth had formed. The bodies in the Solar System formed and evolved with the Sun.

sections.heading: Physical characteristics
sections.content: Earth's shape is approximately an oblate spheroid. The rotation of Earth creates a bulge at the equator, resulting in a diameter about 43 kilometers larger there than at its poles.

sections.heading: Atmosphere
sections.content: The atmospheric pressure at Earth's sea level averages 101.325 kPa, with a scale height of about 8.5 km. A dry atmosphere is composed of 78.084% nitrogen, 20.946% oxygen, 0.934% argon.
